Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected fault on our underground cables that affected 924 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Roland at 09:01 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Hockley SS5 area and are investigating the fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 638 customers. There are now 152 properties still affected. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Roland at 08:48.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 08:27. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. This work is taking place near Station Road.

This work is taking place in the Petham Court area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future. This work is taking place in the Naccolt area.

Steps.Message:
Hi, Bev from the messaging team here; just a quick recap on what's happened during the power cut so far. Our engineers are still on site in the Prince Road area. We can confirm the fault has been caused by third party theft to our equipment. Due to the complexity of the fault we require access to each individual property so we can safely carry out repairs.
 
If you havent yet provided access, you can do so by giving us a call on 0800316105 or just dial 105 and our team will be able to help you.
 
I'm sorry for the continued disruption, but I'll keep you informed with news from site.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Just an update from Bev at 10:37. I hope you're holding up okay without your power, I know things can be more difficult when the lights are out, and we want to help however we can. Food is being served at outside 16 Hurlestone Road (https://what3words.com/guises.youth.park) for households affected by the power cut, so please feel free to make your way there for something to eat and drink. Our Customer Support Vehicle is still also nearby 1 Prince Road to help if you have any questions about the outage today. If you need any other support or want to speak with us, please call us on 08003163105 to chat to one of our team. Otherwise, I'll be in touch once we have some more news.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We've sent our customer support vehicle out to your area. This can provide hot water, some hot food (or a microwave to heat your own), WIFI, charging points and on site information. IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified a piece of equipment called a transformer and an electricity pole that needs replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off and upgrade the transformer and pole. We will remove the existing one and install a new one on the same day. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided. This work is taking place in the Coombes Road area.

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off.

Steps.Message: Sent by Roland at 13:02 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is a fault on our overhead cables that initially interrupted power to 1697 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 333 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 15:30 and 16:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.
